codeReview.summary: The feature fixes loader-side per-path REVIEW selection and YAML warning surfacing, but the assembled review request still serializes only one shared rules object and never applies directory-scoped REVIEW content per changed file/path.

issues.description: ReviewRequest still exposes a single top-level `Rules TrustedRules` object and each `Change` lacks any per-file trusted-instruction field. Although `TrustedRules.ReviewForPath()` was added, `Assembler.Assemble()` never calls it when constructing each change, so downstream provider requests cannot know which directory-scoped REVIEW.md applies to which changed file. The implementation therefore does not satisfy the requirement to apply directory-scoped REVIEW.md per changed file/path instead of collapsing to a single run-wide winner.

summary: Fail: one blocking issue remains. Directory-scoped REVIEW.md lookup was added in the loader, but the assembled ReviewRequest still carries only one shared rules object and does not attach per-path instructions to individual changed files, so the feature does not fully implement path-scoped application of REVIEW.md. No non-blocking issues or shared-state gaps were identified from the reviewed evidence.
